name: Shalford Lodge
description: A superb development of thirteen two and three bedroom luxury apartments in a highly desirable location overlooking the spectacular Richmond Park. Each of these stunning new homes will be finished to the highest standard throughout with a specification others can only envy. All Kidbrook Homes are designed for their unique location and to ensure long term enjoyment for each and every one of our customers.

faqs.question: Where is Shalford Lodge?
faqs.answer: Shalford Lodge is located on Kingston Hill, Kingston upon Thames -- one of London's most prestigious residential addresses. Kingston Hill offers excellent access to Richmond Park, Kingston town centre, and Central London via the A3 and rail connections from Surbiton and Kingston stations.

faqs.question: How many apartments are at Shalford Lodge?
faqs.answer: Shalford Lodge comprises 13 luxury apartments, ranging from 2-bedroom to 3-bedroom configurations. Sizes range from 753 to 1,776 square feet.

faqs.question: What are the prices at Shalford Lodge, Kingston Hill?
faqs.answer: Prices at Shalford Lodge start from £595,000 for a 2-bedroom apartment, up to £1,250,000 for the largest 3-bedroom apartments. Several plots are currently available -- contact us for the latest availability.

faqs.question: What transport links are near Shalford Lodge?
faqs.answer: Kingston Hill is well-connected to Central London. Norbiton station (Zone 6, South Western Railway) is nearby, with trains to London Waterloo in approximately 30 minutes. Kingston town centre and the A3 are both easily accessible.

locationInfo.description: Kingston Hill is one of South West London's most sought-after addresses, offering the rare combination of a leafy, village-like setting with fast access to Central London and the Surrey countryside.

highlights.label: Richmond Park
highlights.detail: Walking distance to London's largest Royal Park, with 2,500 acres of open parkland and deer herds

highlights.label: Kingston Town Centre
highlights.detail: A short drive to Kingston's shops, riverside restaurants, and weekly market

highlights.label: Norbiton Station
highlights.detail: South Western Railway services to London Waterloo in approximately 30 minutes

highlights.label: Kingston University
highlights.detail: Kingston Hill campus directly adjacent, with the town centre campus a short distance away

highlights.label: Excellent Schools
highlights.detail: Catchment for outstanding state and independent schools including Holy Cross, Marymount, and Rokeby

highlights.label: River Thames
highlights.detail: Thames towpath walks and Kingston riverside within easy reach

areaGuide.intro: Shalford Lodge is a collection of 13 beautifully appointed two and three bedroom luxury apartments situated on Kingston Hill. This bespoke development enjoys the outstanding backdrop of Richmond Park whilst benefitting from the convenience of excellent road links into London.

sections.title: A Beautiful Setting
sections.content: Kingston Hill is a prestigious area situated a short distance from the active town centre of Kingston upon Thames, which offers a large selection of shops, eateries and delightful scenic walks along the river Thames.

sections.title: Sought After Location
sections.content: Each of these stunning new homes will be finished to the highest standard throughout with a specification others can only envy. All Kidbrook Homes are designed for their unique location and to ensure long term enjoyment for each and every one of our customers.

name: Ardmore Place
description: Ardmore Place is a new landmark development for central Wimbledon. Set in an established, tree-lined avenue of elegant period, bay-fronted family homes and contemporary high-end apartments. The development's cutting-edge design and high specification creates true exclusivity. Situated within half a mile of Wimbledon Station with its frequent and direct service to Waterloo in under 20 minutes, Ardmore Place is perfectly placed for those wanting the best of both worlds - village living with city connectivity.

faqs.question: Where is Ardmore Place, Wimbledon?
faqs.answer: Ardmore Place is located on Hartfield Road in Wimbledon, South West London. It's within walking distance of Wimbledon Village, Wimbledon Common, and Wimbledon station, which offers District line, Thameslink, and South Western Railway services into Central London.

faqs.question: How many apartments are at Ardmore Place?
faqs.answer: Ardmore Place is a boutique development of just 8 apartments, including studios, 1-bedroom, 2-bedroom apartments, and a penthouse. Sizes range from 431 to 1,152 square feet.

faqs.question: What are the prices at Ardmore Place, Wimbledon?
faqs.answer: Prices at Ardmore Place start from £925,000 for a 2-bedroom apartment. A number of plots have already been sold or reserved -- contact us for current availability.

faqs.question: Are show homes open at Ardmore Place?
faqs.answer: Yes, show homes at Ardmore Place are now open for viewing. Contact us on 01483 923693 or register your interest online to arrange a visit.

highlights.label: Wimbledon Village
highlights.detail: Independent cafes, boutiques, and restaurants on the High Street, a short walk away

highlights.label: Wimbledon Common
highlights.detail: Over 1,100 acres of open heathland, woodland walks, and horse riding

highlights.label: Wimbledon Station
highlights.detail: District line, Thameslink, and South Western Railway services - Waterloo in 17 minutes

highlights.label: All England Club
highlights.detail: Home of The Championships, Wimbledon - one of SW19's most iconic landmarks

areaGuide.intro: Ardmore Place is a new landmark development for central Wimbledon. Set in an established, tree-lined avenue of elegant period, bay-fronted family homes and contemporary high-end apartments.

sections.title: Location
sections.content: Wimbledon is a location where life balance is part of the fabric - a bustling town centre, a stylish village, a beautiful common, a generous park with its very own lake and exceptional interconnections. Famous as the home of the annual Wimbledon Championships, the oldest and most prestigious tennis tournament in the world, Wimbledon also offers a European style cafe society, trendy independent boutiques, lively pubs, relaxing bars and many exhilarating places to eat.

sections.title: Out and About
sections.content: Wimbledon Village combines old school charm with modern day elegance - an ideal place for catching up with friends and enjoying a relaxed meal. Many of the village's eateries have pavement-side dining - the perfect spot to watch the world go by. If you're looking for a bit of rest and relaxation or a spot of retail therapy, few towns can rival Wimbledon.

sections.title: Exceptional Connectivity
sections.content: Ardmore Place occupies an enviable position for numerous reasons. Not only is the development in a delightful, tree-lined avenue just moments from the town centre by foot, but Wimbledon station also offers an exceptional, direct service to Clapham Junction in just 7 minutes and London Waterloo in 19 minutes.

areaGuide.schools: King's College School, Wimbledon College, Ursuline High School, Wimbledon School of English, Wimbledon College of Arts

name: Ockford Road
description: Three beautifully crafted detached family homes in the desirable Surrey town of Godalming. Each home offers generous living space with four bedrooms, four bathrooms, and premium specifications throughout.

faqs.question: Where is the Ockford Road development?
faqs.answer: Ockford Road is in Godalming, a charming historic town in Surrey. Godalming offers independent shops, restaurants, and excellent schools, with Godalming station providing fast rail connections to London Waterloo in approximately 45 minutes.

faqs.question: How many homes are at Ockford Road, Godalming?
faqs.answer: Ockford Road comprises just 3 individually designed detached family homes, each offering 4 bedrooms, 4 bathrooms, and 1,919 square feet of living space.

faqs.question: What are the prices at Ockford Road, Godalming?
faqs.answer: Homes at Ockford Road are priced from £1,250,000. With only 3 homes available, this is an exclusive opportunity. Contact us for the latest availability.

locationInfo.description: Godalming is one of Surrey's most desirable market towns, with a picturesque High Street, strong schools, and the Surrey Hills countryside on your doorstep. Ockford Road is a well-regarded residential road south of the town centre.

highlights.label: Godalming High Street

highlights.label: Surrey Hills AONB
highlights.detail: Immediate access to walks, cycling, and riding in an Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty

highlights.label: Godalming Station
highlights.detail: South Western Railway direct to London Waterloo in approximately 45 minutes

highlights.label: Charterhouse School
highlights.detail: One of the UK's leading independent schools, located on the edge of town

highlights.label: Outstanding Schools
highlights.detail: Strong state schools including Godalming College, Broadwater School, and local primaries

highlights.label: River Wey
highlights.detail: Riverside walks along the Wey Navigation towpath from Godalming to Guildford

areaGuide.intro: An exclusive development of three four-bedroom luxury homes in a highly desirable location in the sought after town of Godalming.

sections.title: Situation
sections.content: A collection of three beautifully appointed four-bedroom luxury family homes situated in Godalming. This bespoke development enjoys privacy and seclusion whilst benefitting from the convenience of being close to the Town Centre and excellent road and rail links.

sections.title: Town & Surroundings
sections.content: There is a superb choice of boutiques, shops, and services in Godalming. For a more extensive range of shopping, sporting and leisure facilities, the Cathedral town of Guildford lies only a few miles away offering a comprehensive range of high street shopping, leisure, and cultural amenities. These include a variety of restaurants, two theatres, multiplex cinema, clubs and bars, the G-Live event venue, and the Spectrum Sports Centre.

areaGuide.schools: Charterhouse, St Catherine's, Royal Grammar School, Guildford High School, Prior's Field, St Hilary's, Godalming Sixth Form College

locationInfo.description: Mayford is a charming village on the outskirts of Woking, offering the best of both worlds - rural tranquility with excellent urban connectivity. The Mayford Meadows Nature Reserve provides a beautiful natural backdrop.

highlights.label: Nature Reserve
highlights.detail: Adjacent to Mayford Meadows Nature Reserve with walking trails and wildlife

highlights.label: Woking Town Centre
highlights.detail: Regenerated town centre with extensive shopping, dining and leisure facilities

highlights.label: Woking Station
highlights.detail: Up to 15 trains per hour, fastest service 24 minutes to London Waterloo

highlights.label: Golf
highlights.detail: Choice of clubs including historic Woking Golf Club (1893), Foxhills, Hoebridge

highlights.label: Good Schools
highlights.detail: Well served by state and private schools in the Woking area

highlights.label: Chobham Common
highlights.detail: National Nature Reserve nearby for walking, cycling and riding

areaGuide.intro: A charming development of two and three bedroom family homes nestled in a semi-rural location, just to the north of this popular and sought after village and Nature Reserve.

sections.title: A Beautiful Setting
sections.content: Whitestone Woods is a collection of just eight beautifully appointed two and three bedroom homes situated in the village of Mayford. This bespoke development enjoys the tranquil backdrop of the Mayford Meadows Nature Reserve whilst benefitting from the convenience of excellent road links to both Guildford and Woking.

sections.title: Town and Country Living
sections.content: Woking has 8th Century origins but really developed with the arrival of the Basingstoke Canal in the 18th Century and the railway in the early 19th Century. Both continue to benefit the town. The canal and the River Wey boast scenic stretches of cycle and walking paths along with boating and fishing spots whilst nearby Chobham Common is a National Nature Reserve.

sections.title: The Best of Both Worlds
sections.content: Mayford is a village which retains country charm whilst being within easy access of Woking Town centre. Woking has a regenerated, thriving town centre offering extensive shopping, dining and leisure facilities including The New Victoria Theatre and Cinema, and the Lightbox Gallery. For golfers there is a choice of clubs including Woking Golf Club (one of the oldest in the U.K. founded in 1893), Westhill, Hoebridge, Worplesdon, Chobham and Foxhills.

areaGuide.schools: A good selection of both state and private schools serve the area
